nodes.label: P08_T001
nodes.identity_summary: This person is a highly brand-loyal consumer who prioritizes fragrance and natural ingredients, relying heavily on online reviews and dermatologist recommendations before making any purchase decisions. Once they find a product that works (like their 2-year loyalty to Beauty, Health and Planet), they become extremely resistant to switching, even for significant price increases or social pressure.

nodes.label: extreme-brand-loyalty-once-satisfied
nodes.description: Has used the same body wash (Beauty, Health and Planet) for 2 years and consistently repurchases it despite price increases and alternatives.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Using same bodywash for 2 years. Discovered via positive social media reviews and tried it
[REAL] Very_loyal (brand loyalty rating)
[SYNTHETIC] Pay the extra ₹80 and stick with your trusted brand. I'm very brand loyal and have been satisfied with Beauty, Health and Planet for 2 years
[SYNTHETIC] All the same brand and variant. I've been repurchasing Beauty Health and Planet consistently for 2 years
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new body wash concept, this person would likely dismiss it unless it offers compelling evidence of superiority over their current brand, because their loyalty runs extremely deep once satisfied.

nodes.label: fragrance-as-primary-purchase-driver
nodes.description: Prioritizes fragrance above most other factors, specifically loving floral and sweet gourmand scents, and repurchased current product primarily for its scent.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Love the scent of Beauty, Health and Planet bodywash; repurchased for the scent
[REAL] Floral,sweet_gourmand (preferred fragrance families)
[REAL] Good fragrance, feels natural (what they like most about current product)
[SYNTHETIC] Not try it — fragrance is non-negotiable in body wash. I love the scent of my current body wash and fragrance is one of my top priorities
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash ad emphasizing texture or clinical benefits, this person would likely ask about fragrance first, because scent is their non-negotiable requirement.

nodes.label: review-dependent-decision-maker
nodes.description: Consistently checks star ratings, review counts, and reads multiple reviews before trying any new product, trusting online reviews more than advertising.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] ["star_rating","review_count","brand_name"] (what they look at first when shopping online)
[REAL] {"Online reviews (Amazon, Nykaa)":4} vs {"TV advertisements":1} (trust ratings)
[SYNTHETIC] Brand A. The 15,000 reviews give me more confidence than just 120 reviews, even with the lower rating
[SYNTHETIC] I always check star ratings and review count before buying anything, especially unknown brands
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new product concept, this person would likely immediately search for existing reviews and ratings rather than trust the brand's claims, because they need social proof before purchasing.

nodes.label: natural-ingredients-preference-with-research
nodes.description: Values natural products for long-term skin health but researches ingredients rather than blindly trusting 'natural' claims.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Natural means good for long-term use, free from chemicals with harmful skin effects (rating: 4)
[REAL] Varies by product category; mostly prefer long-term sustainable products
[SYNTHETIC] Natural means good for long-term use to me, and I prefer products free from chemicals with harmful skin effects
[SYNTHETIC] Research the specific flagged ingredients. I'd want to understand what the app considers problematic before deciding
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a 'natural' body wash concept, this person would likely research the specific ingredients and their benefits rather than accept the natural claim at face value, because they want genuine long-term skin benefits.

nodes.label: dermatologist-as-highest-trust-source
nodes.description: Rates dermatologists as the most trustworthy source (5/5) for product information and actively seeks their recommendations.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] {"Dermatologists or skin doctors":5} (highest trust rating)
[REAL] ["doctor_rec","friend_rec","influencer"] (what would make them switch)
[SYNTHETIC] Trust the dermatologist since it's their field. I rated dermatologists 5/5 for trustworthiness in product information
[SYNTHETIC] Dermatologist's Instagram reel. I trust dermatologists most for product information
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash concept endorsed by dermatologists, this person would likely be highly receptive and prioritize it over other options, because dermatologist credibility is their gold standard.

nodes.label: premium-price-tolerance-for-quality
nodes.description: Spends above ₹500 on body wash and willing to pay premiums for proven quality, preferring one good product over multiple cheaper options.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Above_500 (typical spending on body wash)
[REAL] Around 2000 (paid for Bath and Body Works)
[SYNTHETIC] Buy one premium body wash at ₹450-500. I prefer spending more on quality products that last longer
[SYNTHETIC] One bottle of a premium brand I love (₹499). I prefer spending on quality products that I know work well
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a premium body wash concept at ₹600+, this person would likely consider it seriously if it demonstrates clear quality benefits, because they already spend premium amounts for products they trust.

nodes.label: social-media-product-discovery-reliance
nodes.description: Discovers new products primarily through Instagram and YouTube influencers, particularly valuing unpaid/organic reviews.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] ["instagram","youtube","influencer"] (where they discover new products)
[REAL] Unpaid/organic product promotions (what makes influencers trustworthy)
[REAL] Yes, multiple unpaid reviews led to purchase
[SYNTHETIC] I discover products through Instagram and YouTube influencers, so I'd look for influencer recommendations
nodes.predictive_value: When a new body wash concept is promoted through authentic influencer content rather than paid ads, this person would likely pay attention and research further, because organic social media is their primary discovery channel.

nodes.label: amazon-nykaa-platform-loyalty
nodes.description: Strongly prefers shopping on Amazon and Nykaa over other platforms, even willing to pay more for platform familiarity and trust.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] ["amazon","quick_commerce","nykaa"] (top 3 shopping channels)
[SYNTHETIC] Amazon link. I trust Amazon's review system and return policy more, plus I'm used to shopping there
[SYNTHETIC] Wait until they're available on Amazon or Nykaa. I prefer established platforms for security and convenience
[SYNTHETIC] Amazon/Flipkart. Best selection, reliable reviews and ratings, good prices
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash concept available only on a brand's own website, this person would likely wait until it appears on Amazon or Nykaa rather than purchase directly, because platform trust matters more than immediate availability.

nodes.label: texture-preference-for-thick-creamy-formulas
nodes.description: Specifically prefers thick, creamy body wash textures and wants moisturized post-wash skin feeling.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Thick_cream (preferred texture)
[REAL] Moisturized (ideal post-wash skin feeling)
[REAL] Bodywash: more hygienic, more convenient, smells better (why they choose bodywash over soap)
[SYNTHETIC] Being able to choose my preferred floral scent and creamy texture while adding beneficial ingredients sounds worth ₹599
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a gel or lightweight body wash concept, this person would likely be less interested compared to cream-based formulations, because they specifically seek rich, moisturizing textures.

nodes.label: packaging-aesthetics-matter-for-premium-feel
nodes.description: Prefers luxurious packaging and is willing to pay extra for premium-looking bottles, though criticizes current brand's packaging.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Luxurious (packaging preference)
[REAL] Packaging could be better (frustration with current product)
[REAL] Good floral or vanilla fragrance, creamy texture, premium packaging, price around 500 (ideal product description)
[SYNTHETIC] The sleek bottle at ₹399. I prefer luxurious packaging and am willing to pay extra for it
nodes.predictive_value: When shown two identical body wash concepts with different packaging, this person would likely choose the more premium-looking option even at higher cost, because packaging aesthetics influence their perception of quality.

nodes.label: clinical-claims-skepticism-needs-proof
nodes.description: Values clinical testing and dermatologist recommendations but wants to see actual studies and proof rather than just claims.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] ["clinical_studies","derm_recommended","shea_vitamin_e"] (claims that make them read more)
[REAL] {"Clinical-grade, lab-tested formulation":"20_40"} (willing to pay 20-40% premium)
[SYNTHETIC] I'd need to see the clinical study results first. Same ingredients means I need proof that the testing adds real value
[SYNTHETIC] The second product with the detailed blog post. I prefer transparent information over vague claims
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash concept with clinical claims, this person would likely demand to see the actual studies and methodology rather than trust the claims alone, because they want substantiated proof of benefits.

nodes.label: convenience-over-small-savings
nodes.description: Willing to pay small premiums (₹20-50) for convenience and familiar shopping platforms rather than hunt for best prices.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Amazon for ₹319. Free Prime delivery is convenient and the ₹20 extra is worth avoiding the trip to the store
[SYNTHETIC] Buy it on Nykaa right now. The convenience is worth ₹50 and I prefer online shopping
[SYNTHETIC] Order from Amazon tomorrow. I'm very loyal to my preferred brand and would rather wait than compromise
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash concept available at slightly different prices across platforms, this person would likely choose their preferred platform even if it costs ₹20-50 more, because convenience trumps small savings.

nodes.label: friend-family-recommendations-high-influence
nodes.description: Rates friends and family as highly trustworthy (4/5) and would be influenced by their recommendations, especially close relationships.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] {"Friends and family":4} (trust rating)
[REAL] ["doctor_rec","friend_rec","influencer"] (what would make them switch)
[SYNTHETIC] My close friend's recommendation. Friends and family rank higher in my trust sources
[SYNTHETIC] Go with the recommendation from the person whose opinion you trust most. I value trusted sources over popular consensus
nodes.predictive_value: When a close friend recommends a new body wash concept, this person would likely research it seriously and potentially try it, because personal relationships carry significant weight in their decision-making.

nodes.label: resistance-to-subscription-models
nodes.description: Prefers flexibility in purchasing and dislikes being locked into subscriptions, even for discounts.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Option A: ₹349 one-time purchase. I don't like being locked into subscriptions, and I prefer controlling when I buy my products
[SYNTHETIC] Don't subscribe — I like the freedom to switch brands anytime. Even though I'm very brand loyal, I want the option to try something new
[SYNTHETIC] Switch to a different brand I can buy freely. I'm very loyal to brands but prefer the flexibility of buying when I want
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash subscription offer with significant discounts, this person would likely decline in favor of one-time purchases, because they value purchasing flexibility over savings.

nodes.label: sample-testing-before-commitment
nodes.description: Prefers to test products in smaller sizes or samples before committing to full bottles, especially for premium products.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] When I want to try a new variant before committing (when they'd buy sachets)
[SYNTHETIC] Buy a ₹99 mini size from Nykaa first. I need to test the fragrance and texture before committing to a premium product
[SYNTHETIC] Ask for a sample first before committing. I'd support my friend but need to test if it works for my skin before spending ₹450
[SYNTHETIC] Buy the sachet to try — perfect low-risk trial. It's a great way to test without committing to the full bottle price
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new premium body wash concept, this person would likely look for trial sizes or samples first rather than buying the full product, because they need to test compatibility before investing.

nodes.label: social-validation-reinforces-choices

[SYNTHETIC] I'd definitely repurchase the same product — the compliment reinforces my choice. External validation about how I smell to others matters to me
[SYNTHETIC] I feel validated and more loyal to the brand. Social validation reinforces my product choices
[SYNTHETIC] More confident in my choice — clearly it's popular for a reason. If other people are discovering what I already know is good, that validates my decision
nodes.predictive_value: When others compliment a body wash concept this person is using, they would likely become even more loyal to it and recommend it to others, because social validation strongly reinforces their product choices.

nodes.label: moderate-novelty-openness-with-research
nodes.description: Rates themselves as moderately comfortable (3/5) trying new brands but always researches thoroughly before switching.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] 3 (comfort level trying new brands)
[SYNTHETIC] I'm moderately open to trying new brands, especially with personalized recommendations
[SYNTHETIC] The exclusivity is appealing but I need some brand trust first
[SYNTHETIC] I'd be curious but wait for others to validate the rebrand first. I'd want to see reviews and proof that the changes are genuine
nodes.predictive_value: When shown an innovative new body wash concept, this person would likely be curious and research it extensively, but would wait for reviews and social proof before trying, because they balance openness with caution.

nodes.label: ingredient-awareness-but-not-expert
nodes.description: Recognizes some key ingredients like sulfates but isn't an expert, preferring to research unfamiliar ingredients before deciding.

[SYNTHETIC] Research the specific flagged ingredients. I'd want to understand what the app considers problematic before deciding
[SYNTHETIC] Google 'skin microbiome' to understand before buying. I research ingredients I don't understand, especially since I care about long-term skin effects
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash concept with unfamiliar active ingredients, this person would likely research those ingredients online before purchasing, because they want to understand what they're putting on their skin without being intimidated by complexity.

nodes.label: gifting-requires-premium-presentation

[SYNTHETIC] A premium international brand (Bath & Body Works, The Body Shop) — looks impressive. For gifting, luxurious packaging matters
[SYNTHETIC] The premium one — I want my guests to have a good experience. Good hospitality matters and they should experience quality products

nodes.label: seasonal-product-interest-with-testing

[SYNTHETIC] Try it only in a small/travel size first. I prefer testing new product categories in smaller sizes before committing

nodes.label: multi-benefit-integration-appreciator

[SURVEY] custom formulation based on different body areas is very appealing since it addresses personalized skincare needs

links.mechanism: Their extreme loyalty developed after discovering their current brand through positive social media reviews, making them rely heavily on reviews for any future switching decisions since that method worked so well initially.

While they're extremely loyal and repurchase the same product consistently, they still want the psychological freedom to change their mind, creating tension between their actual behavior and desired flexibility.
Since fragrance is non-negotiable and highly personal, they need to smell and experience products before committing, especially for premium purchases where fragrance disappointment would be costly.
Their preference for natural, long-term safe products drives them to research ingredients they don't understand, building basic knowledge without becoming a chemistry expert.

Their high trust in dermatologists makes them expect the same level of scientific rigor from clinical claims, demanding actual studies rather than marketing copy because they know what real expertise looks like.
Their willingness to spend ₹500+ on body wash creates an expectation that premium-priced products should look premium too, making packaging aesthetics part of their quality assessment.

Their discovery through social media creates a need to validate influencer recommendations with broader review data, using multiple unpaid reviews to confirm what they initially saw on Instagram/YouTube.
Their strong preference for familiar platforms makes them willing to pay small premiums to avoid the friction of learning new shopping interfaces or trusting unfamiliar websites.

Their positive response to compliments about their current choices makes them more receptive to recommendations from people whose opinions they value, since social feedback has proven meaningful to them.
Their moderate comfort with new products combined with their research-heavy approach creates a preference for low-risk trial methods that let them test before fully committing to unfamiliar brands.
Because fragrance is the most important factor, this person is drawn to concepts that offer fragrance customization, making them willing to pay premium for personalized scent experiences.
Since fragrance is so critical and personal, this person needs to test scents before committing to full-size products, driving their consistent request for trial options.
Because fragrance is most important, this person has developed sophisticated preferences about scent balance and intensity, making them sensitive to overwhelming fragrances.
Despite expressing price concerns for every concept, they're willing to pay 599 rupees for their preferred personalized option, showing that fragrance customization overcomes price sensitivity.
Wanting trial sizes before committing to full products supports their price sensitivity by allowing them to test before investing in expensive full-size versions.
Their appreciation for innovation combined with natural skepticism drives them to seek professional validation and proof for tech-enabled concepts before commitment.

While satisfied with their current product, the appeal of personalized fragrance experiences creates enough motivation to consider switching, showing personalization can overcome satisfaction inertia.
Daily usage means frequent repurchasing, making cost-effectiveness calculations more important and driving their focus on value for money.
Their need for detailed fragrance information reinforces fragrance as the primary decision factor, creating a feedback loop of scent-focused evaluation.
Preferring products that combine multiple benefits helps justify higher prices by delivering more value per rupee spent, supporting their value-conscious mindset.
